data.description:
## Why

Three hygiene items from audit OSS finding #6's cleanup bucket:

1. .joe-janitor/ internal agent-tooling reports are committed to the public mirror (tools.md and report-*.md are tracked) — internal-process noise for outsiders. Decision: remove the tracked reports from the index and ignore the directory **locally only** (.git/info/exclude, not the shared .gitignore), so the tooling keeps working on this machine without advertising the path in the public tree.
2. .tokensave/ (code-graph MCP cache) is untracked and unignored — it pollutes git status on any machine running the tokensave server. Add it to the shared .gitignore alongside .serena/ (both are per-developer tool caches, so shared ignore is correct here).
3. docs/api-stability.md hardcodes "currently 0.6.0" while the package is 0.7.1 — a stale version in the stability-contract doc. Replace the literal with wording that does not need per-release maintenance (or a pointer to PyPI).

## What / files

- git rm --cached .joe-janitor/ tracked files; add .joe-janitor/ to .git/info/exclude (NOT .gitignore)
- .gitignore: add .tokensave/ (and dedupe the duplicated dist/ + build/ entries while in the file — audit low finding)
- docs/api-stability.md: remove/replace the stale hardcoded version

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] git ls-files shows nothing under .joe-janitor/; the directory persists on disk and is excluded via .git/info/exclude only
- [ ] .tokensave/ ignored by the shared .gitignore; git status clean with the tokensave server active
- [ ] .gitignore no longer duplicates dist/ and build/
- [ ] docs/api-stability.md contains no hardcoded current-version literal (grep-verifiable)
data.title: Repo hygiene: untrack .joe-janitor reports (local-only exclude), gitignore .tokensave, fix api-stability version
